goal: Phase gate evaluation engine with server-side metric computation, phaseOrder update for CUTOVER_IN_PROGRESS/GO_LIVE, and notification triggers for gate readiness, drift, and certification events

acceptance_criteria.description: GateEvaluationResult model in models/types.go: passed (bool), metrics (map[string]GateMetricResult), blocking_failures ([]string — human-readable descriptions of why the gate failed). GateMetricResult: name (string), current_value (float64), threshold (float64), passed (bool), description (string). Gate metric definitions are hardcoded constants — not configurable per engagement. Metrics evaluated per phase transition: RECONCILING to PARALLEL_RUN requires mapping_agreed_pct >= 0.90. PARALLEL_RUN to COMPLETE requires recon_gate_score >= 0.95 AND recon_p1_unresolved == 0. COMPLETE to CUTOVER_IN_PROGRESS requires has_completed_parallel_run == true AND has_certification == true.

EvaluateGate function in api/gate_evaluator.go: EvaluateGate(db *sql.DB, engagementID string, fromPhase, toPhase models.EngagementStatus) (*models.GateEvaluationResult, error). Updates GetGateMetrics to incorporate M09b recon execution results: the recon_gate_score metric uses the most recent completed ReconExecutionRun's match ratio (match_count / total_evaluated) from db.GetReconExecutionSummary. The legacy reconciliation table query is retained as a separate metric (recon_legacy_score) for backward compatibility. Also update phaseOrder and orderedPhases arrays in gate_handlers.go to include CUTOVER_IN_PROGRESS and GO_LIVE — these are prerequisites for the gate evaluator to support the full status chain. Returns GateEvaluationResult with per-metric pass/fail and blocking failures list. The function is deterministic — same DB state always produces same result.

Phase advance handler (existing POST /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/advance) updated to call EvaluateGate before allowing the transition. If evaluation fails (passed=false) and no overrides are provided, returns 422 with {evaluation: GateEvaluationResult, message: 'Gate requirements not met'}. If overrides array in request body contains the names of failing metrics (e.g., ['recon_gate_score']), the transition proceeds with overrides recorded in gate_transition.overrides JSONB. GET /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/gate-evaluation?target_phase={phase} returns the evaluation result without performing any transition — preview mode for the UI.

Notification triggers using db.CreateNotification (from M05b). Four trigger points wired into existing handlers: (1) Drift critical: drift executor calls CreateNotification when critical_changes > 0 (ensure wired if not already from M05b). (2) Recon failure: recon_execution executor calls CreateNotification when p1_count > 0, type='RECON_P1_DETECTED', summary='Recon execution found {N} P1 mismatches in {engagement_name}'. (3) Gate readiness: after any recon_execution or certification completes, check if all gate metrics pass for the next phase — if so, CreateNotification type='GATE_READY', summary='{engagement_name} meets requirements to advance to {next_phase}'. (4) Certification: certification_handlers.go calls CreateNotification type='CERTIFIED', summary='{engagement_name} certified by {user} with gate score {score}'.

GET /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/gate-status updated to include evaluation result alongside existing metrics and AI recommendation. Response shape: {metrics: map, recommendation: AIRecommendation, evaluation: GateEvaluationResult}. The evaluation field shows whether the engagement currently meets gate requirements for the natural next phase (derived from ValidTransitions). If no valid next phase (GO_LIVE is terminal), evaluation is null.

critical: Gate metric thresholds are hardcoded constants — not user-configurable, ensures consistent governance
Override mechanism requires explicit metric names — the user must acknowledge each failing metric
Overrides are recorded in gate_transition — full audit trail of bypassed gate requirements
EvaluateGate is a pure function of DB state — deterministic, testable, no side effects
Notification triggers are non-blocking — CreateNotification failures do not fail the parent operation
phaseOrder and orderedPhases updated for CUTOVER_IN_PROGRESS and GO_LIVE — enables full status chain
high: Gate evaluation preview endpoint (GET ?target_phase=) — UI can show pass/fail before user attempts advance
Notification type values are documented constants — UI can filter and style by type
Gate readiness check runs after recon execution and certification — catches the moment requirements are met
WebSocket broadcast notification_created on all notification inserts — real-time bell updates
medium: blocking_failures array is human-readable — can be shown directly in UI without parsing
Gate metrics for each phase transition are documented in code comments — auditable governance
Evaluation includes current_value and threshold per metric — UI can show progress bars
